metadata.title: hardware - Network Switches (Core and Access Layer)

components.title: Network Switches (Core and Access Layer)
components.description: Cisco Catalyst series switches deployed as core (Layer 3) and access (Layer 2) network switches. Core switches provide inter-VLAN routing and connection to the firewall. Access switches provide port-level security via IEEE 802.1X, VLAN assignment, and port security. Configuration managed via Cisco DNA Center or manual CLI with version-controlled backups.
components.purpose: Provide VLAN-segmented, port-secured network connectivity for all systems.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Network Switches (Core and Access Layer).

implemented-requirements.description: VLANs are configured on access switches to enforce network segmentation: Corporate VLAN, Research VLAN, Management VLAN, DMZ VLAN, Guest VLAN, and VoIP VLAN. Inter-VLAN routing is controlled by the core switch ACLs and enforced by the firewall. VLAN assignments are reviewed quarterly.

IEEE 802.1X port-based Network Access Control (NAC) is enforced on all access switch ports: domain-joined devices authenticate via machine certificate (EAP-TLS); non-domain devices are placed in a guest VLAN with internet-only access. Unauthorized devices cannot access the corporate network.

Switch hardening is applied: SSH v2 only for management (Telnet disabled), SNMP v3 with AES encryption, unused ports administratively shut down and assigned to a blackhole VLAN, spanning tree PortFast and BPDU Guard on access ports, DHCP snooping, Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI), and IP source guard enabled on all access VLANs.

Switch syslog is forwarded to the SIEM: all configuration changes (CONFIG_I), port state changes (LINK-3-UPDOWN), spanning tree events, 802.1X authentication success/failure, and MAC address table changes for designated high-security ports. NTP synchronized to the organizational time server.

Core switches are deployed in redundant pairs with Virtual Switching System (VSS) or StackWise for active-active redundancy and sub-second failover. Access switches are dual-homed to the core switch pair. Switch configurations are backed up nightly to a TFTP server and to the CMDB.
